name: Illinois Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Illinois - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Division of Professional Regulation — Speech-Language Pathology
provider.parentOrganization: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR)

address.streetAddress: 320 W. Washington St., 3rd Floor, Springfield, IL 62786
address.description:
Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation
320 W. Washington Street
Springfield, IL 62786

requiredDocuments.name: Official graduate transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: From an IDFPR-approved or ASHA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

requiredDocuments.name: Clinical Fellowship verification
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation of completed CFY signed by supervising licensed SLP.

requiredDocuments.name: Background check
requiredDocuments.description: Criminal background check as required by IDFPR.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does an Illinois SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application fee is $90 (by examination) or $100 (by endorsement). A Temporary SLP License costs $75. Biennial renewal is $100, due by October 31 of odd-numbered years.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a PEL to work as an SLP in Illinois schools?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. SLPs working in Illinois public schools must hold a Professional Educator License (PEL) from the Illinois State Board of Education in addition to the IDFPR professional SLP license.

mainEntity.name: What continuing education is required for Illinois SLP renewal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Illinois requires 20 hours of CE every two years, including 1 hour of ethics, 1 hour of sexual harassment training, 1 hour of implicit bias awareness, 1 hour of dementia recognition training (every 6 years), and 1 hour of cultural competency training (effective January 1, 2025).

mainEntity.name: Does Illinois have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Illinois. To work in Illinois, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Illinois?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Illinois while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
